title: The Deep South

jokes.text: Dream on! I want to put him in a sack, toss the sack in the river... and hurl the river into space.
jokes.setup: Leela thinks Hermes cares about Nibbler getting a pet license
jokes.punchline: Hermes reveals an absurdly elaborate plan to get rid of Nibbler, escalating from sack to river to space
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect absurdist logic. The escalation from sack→river→space is beautifully constructed and purely Hermes-like bureaucratic overkill.

jokes.text: But I like filling out requisitions. And these were some doozies!
jokes.setup: Hermes just described wanting to destroy Nibbler
jokes.punchline: He reveals his real motivation was just enjoying paperwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - only Hermes would get excited about paperwork. Good follow-up to the previous joke.

jokes.text: Great Jah's dreadlocks! There's been a mix-up.
jokes.setup: Standard expletive situation
jokes.punchline: Hermes uses a Rastafarian-themed exclamation fitting his Jamaican heritage
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific exclamation that fits Hermes' background, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Seems the logical place for fish to congregate!
jokes.setup: They're in the exact center of the Atlantic Ocean
jokes.punchline: Professor states obvious logic as if it's profound insight
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Professor's tendency to state obvious things scientifically.

jokes.text: Falcon, this is Blue Raven. The goose has nested.
jokes.setup: They're on a simple fishing trip
jokes.punchline: Bender treats it like a spy operation with elaborate code names
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's tendency to overcomplicate and dramatize simple situations.

jokes.text: Guess what you're accessories to? The sun, the sea air, good friends.
jokes.setup: Bender asks what they're accessories to, implying something criminal
jokes.punchline: He lists innocent, pleasant things instead of crimes
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that subverts Bender's criminal nature for a surprisingly wholesome moment.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? I once caught a fish this big.
jokes.setup: Someone claims to have caught a big fish
jokes.punchline: Fry one-ups them with presumably an even more exaggerated gesture
jokes.explanation: Standard fish story escalation - predictable but functional.

jokes.text: Harpoon my ass! Okay!
jokes.setup: Leela dismissively says 'Harpoon my ass!'
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately agrees to do exactly that
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Takes a throwaway expression literally for a quick laugh.

jokes.text: These sardines, for example, are bland and tasteless.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg offers to be a 'bait critic'
jokes.punchline: He's obviously just trying to get free food while pretending to be helpful
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg move - transparent attempt to get food while being 'helpful.'

jokes.text: Very, very juicy.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg is 'critiquing' night crawlers
jokes.punchline: He emphasizes how juicy they are, clearly savoring eating them
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out moment with Zoidberg's disgusting eating habits on full display.

jokes.text: I don't believe this! It's eating my bait! Beat it, you mooching crawdad!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg has been eating all the bait
jokes.punchline: Fry calls him a 'mooching crawdad' - technically accurate since Zoidberg is a lobster-like creature
jokes.explanation: Clever insult that works on multiple levels - both as generic insult and species-specific accuracy.

jokes.text: I can hit a fish between the eyes from 20 yards. Yeah? I can hit a shrimp!
jokes.setup: Bender brags about precision fishing
jokes.punchline: Fry tries to one-up him but chooses a much smaller, easier target
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of typical one-upmanship where Fry accidentally admits to less impressive skill.

jokes.text: So, this is where you shop for your boots!
jokes.setup: Fry has caught a tiny fish
jokes.punchline: Bender sarcastically suggests it's smaller than a boot
jokes.explanation: Standard small fish joke with Bender's sarcastic delivery.

jokes.text: A couple more of those and you'll have yourself a fish stick.
jokes.setup: Fry's fish is tiny
jokes.punchline: Leela suggests multiple tiny fish would make one fish stick
jokes.explanation: Continuation of small fish running gag, but less creative than previous entries.

jokes.text: My small intestine. Bingo!
jokes.setup: Someone asks what Zoidberg caught
jokes.punchline: He's caught his own internal organ somehow
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and uniquely Zoidberg. The casual 'Bingo!' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: What you need is this fish pheromone. The most potent aphrodisiac known to fishkind.
jokes.setup: Amy offers to help with fishing
jokes.punchline: She has fish pheromones, treating fish seduction like human dating
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept treating fishing like dating, fits Amy's character.

jokes.text: I am so into you!
jokes.setup: Amy uses fish pheromones
jokes.punchline: A fish becomes romantically attracted and speaks
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the pheromone setup, treating fish like horny human.

jokes.text: Bender, I just caught a fish this big! Quit exaggerating!
jokes.setup: Fry excitedly shows off his catch
jokes.punchline: Bender tells him to quit exaggerating, implying it's even smaller than Fry claims
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of small fish running gag with Bender's typical deflation of others' enthusiasm.

jokes.text: If I'm not gonna catch a fish, I might as well not catch a big fish.
jokes.setup: Fry is frustrated with fishing
jokes.punchline: His logic about failure is charmingly backwards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - endearingly backwards reasoning that somehow makes sense to him.

jokes.text: There. Like most problems, this one can be solved with bending.
jokes.setup: Bender decides to use his bending power for fishing
jokes.punchline: He treats his one skill as a universal solution
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - his supreme confidence that bending solves everything is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Then, why do I have to be careful? It belonged to my grandmother.
jokes.setup: Leela warns Bender to be careful with unbreakable tether
jokes.punchline: When Bender logically asks why be careful if it's unbreakable, Leela reveals sentimental value
jokes.explanation: Good logical setup with emotional payoff. Bender's question is perfectly reasonable.

jokes.text: My Manwich!
jokes.setup: Something chaotic is happening on the boat
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's priority is his canned meat product
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg priorities - caring more about food than danger. Short and effective.

jokes.text: I wasn't wearing it. I was eating it!
jokes.setup: Someone asks when Zoidberg wears boots
jokes.punchline: He clarifies he eats boots instead of wearing them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his alien perspective on human clothing as food source.

jokes.text: What happened to my parasol? It wasn't here when I took your umbrella.
jokes.setup: Amy asks about her missing parasol
jokes.punchline: Leela casually admits she took Amy's umbrella to replace it
jokes.explanation: Good casual admission of petty theft. Leela's matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Sweet zombie Jesus! It's huge!
jokes.setup: Something massive appears
jokes.punchline: Professor uses his signature blasphemous exclamation with zombie twist
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor exclamation. 'Sweet zombie Jesus' is highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Well, at least I'll die with my friends. Hello?
jokes.setup: Leela tries to find comfort in dying with friends
jokes.punchline: She realizes she's completely alone
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of 'dying with dignity' trope. The quiet 'Hello?' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: How many atmospheres can the ship withstand? It's a spaceship, so I'd say anywhere between zero and one.
jokes.setup: Crisis about deep-sea pressure
jokes.punchline: Professor reveals spaceships are designed for vacuum, not pressure
jokes.explanation: Excellent scientific logic joke. Shows the fundamental problem with using a spaceship underwater.

jokes.text: What's that? Is someone bending girders?
jokes.setup: The ship is making concerning structural sounds under pressure
jokes.punchline: Bender optimistically thinks it might be his kind of work happening
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender hearing disaster sounds and thinking of his profession.

jokes.text: Tempers are wearing thin. Let's hope some robot doesn't kill everybody!
jokes.setup: Bender comments on the tense survival situation
jokes.punchline: He expresses hope that no robot kills everyone while being the only robot present
jokes.explanation: Good self-referential humor with Bender's fake concern about robot violence.

jokes.text: I suggest a nice lobster Zoidberg. I mean lobster Newburg. I mean Dr. Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Bender suggests what to eat for lunch
jokes.punchline: He 'accidentally' suggests eating Zoidberg, corrects to lobster dish, then corrects back
jokes.explanation: Excellently structured cannibalism joke with perfect 'accidental' slips showing Bender's true thoughts.

jokes.text: Not necessarily. This is a chance for Fry to test my experimental anti-pressure pill.
jokes.setup: Warning that pressure will crush Fry
jokes.punchline: Professor sees it as an opportunity to test experimental drugs on Fry
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about using crew as test subjects. Very Professor-like disregard for safety.

jokes.text: I can't swallow that! Well, then, good news! It's a suppository.
jokes.setup: Fry can't swallow the large pill
jokes.punchline: Professor cheerfully reveals the alternative delivery method
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor 'good news/bad news' reversal. The enthusiastic delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm Dr. Zoidberg, homeowner!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg finds shelter underwater
jokes.punchline: He's pathetically excited about the most basic achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his excitement over basic achievements highlights his usual failures.

jokes.text: The laws of science be a harsh mistress.
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation about underwater situation
jokes.punchline: Bender delivers pseudo-philosophical pirate speak
jokes.explanation: Good Bender line mixing pirate speech with science. Quotable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Okay. But I'm not carrying it upstairs.
jokes.setup: Someone asks Bender to help move a couch underwater
jokes.punchline: He agrees but sets a reasonable boundary about stairs
jokes.explanation: Good understated humor - practical concern about moving furniture underwater.

jokes.text: Then how about lending your old pal Zoidberg a few bucks, Mr. Millionaire.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg learns Fry's student loans are repaid
jokes.punchline: He immediately hits up Fry for money based on this assumption
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg opportunism - immediately trying to benefit from others' good fortune.

jokes.text: A mermaid! You think you saw a mermaid? No, I did see a mermaid. She was wearing a tube top... and she had a scaly tail, and hair extensions.
jokes.setup: Fry claims to have seen a mermaid
jokes.punchline: He describes her with modern fashion accessories
jokes.explanation: Good modernization of mythical creature with contemporary fashion elements.

jokes.text: Fry is suffering from ocean madness. Every time something good happens to me, you say it's madness. Or I'm drunk. Or I ate too much candy.
jokes.setup: Professor diagnoses Fry with ocean madness
jokes.punchline: Fry points out the pattern of his friends' dismissive explanations
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about the show's running gag of explaining away Fry's claims.

jokes.text: He may have ocean madness, but that's no excuse for ocean rudeness.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Fry's ocean madness
jokes.punchline: Hermes creates parallel 'ocean rudeness' concept for bad manners
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay creating 'ocean rudeness' to parallel 'ocean madness.' Very quotable.

jokes.text: Thanks. They're called pants.
jokes.setup: Mermaid compliments Fry's 'wiggly doodads' from his hips
jokes.punchline: Fry matter-of-factly explains pants to someone who's never seen them
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash humor with Fry's casual explanation of basic human clothing.

jokes.text: Sure, sometimes it falls from the sky. And sometimes it doesn't.
jokes.setup: Mermaid asks if there's water on land
jokes.punchline: Fry gives the most basic, unhelpful description of rain
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Fry's terrible explanation skills and literal thinking.

jokes.text: Yep! We call them land sea lions. I tame them.
jokes.setup: Mermaid asks about sea lions on land
jokes.punchline: Fry creates the redundant term 'land sea lions' and claims to tame them
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd terminology and Fry's casual lying to impress. 'Land sea lions' is brilliantly ridiculous.

jokes.text: In the event of emergency, my ass can be used as flotation device.
jokes.setup: Discussion of oxygen for underwater search
jokes.punchline: Amy offers her posterior as emergency equipment
jokes.explanation: Moderately crude humor that works for Amy's character but relies on body comedy.

jokes.text: Bend me! Dude! An ancient sunken city!
jokes.setup: Discovery of underwater city
jokes.punchline: Bender uses his own name as an exclamation plus surfer speak
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific exclamation mixing Bender's ego with casual slang.

jokes.text: It is! It's the fabled lost city of Atlanta!
jokes.setup: Discovery of legendary lost underwater city
jokes.punchline: It's Atlanta - a mundane modern American city
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of lost city trope. Atlanta is perfect choice - known but unromantic.

jokes.text: This is uncomfortable and humiliating! Now, if they could put it in the form of a suppository...
jokes.setup: Professor complains about breathing apparatus
jokes.punchline: He prefers the suppository delivery method from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier suppository joke, showing Professor's consistent strange preferences.

jokes.text: I think it was just an airport. They had a place to buy nuts.
jokes.setup: Question about Atlanta's historical significance
jokes.punchline: Fry reduces major city to airport with nut stand
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Atlanta's importance through Fry's limited perspective and poor memory.

jokes.text: Normally, yes. But the caffeine really sped things up.
jokes.setup: Question about evolution taking millions of years
jokes.punchline: Caffeine (Coca-Cola) accelerated evolution
jokes.explanation: Clever pseudo-scientific explanation linking Coca-Cola to rapid evolution. Good Atlanta reference.

jokes.text: She's right. Sure they got the Braves, but it's a third-rate symphony.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Fry belongs in Atlanta
jokes.punchline: Bender gives mixed review focusing on cultural amenities
jokes.explanation: Good specific Atlanta reference mixing sports and culture in typical city review fashion.

jokes.text: Realize it? I don't even understand it!
jokes.setup: Professor asks if Fry realizes the consequences of staying
jokes.punchline: Fry admits complete lack of comprehension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - honest about his confusion rather than pretending to understand.

jokes.text: Sorry, I'm trying to join the country club.
jokes.setup: Mermaid invites Fry to visit her place
jokes.punchline: Fry mentions completely unrelated social aspiration
jokes.explanation: Random non-sequitur showing Fry's scattered priorities and social climbing attempts.

jokes.text: How did this happen? That's a good question! So that's where I left my cigar. That just raises further questions!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg's house burned down underwater, Hermes asks how
jokes.punchline: Bender casually admits causing it, Professor notes this creates more questions
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity - fire underwater raises questions, Bender's admission raises more.

jokes.text: That just raises further questions!
jokes.setup: Bender explains his cigar caused the fire
jokes.punchline: Hermes points out this explanation makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good logical reaction to absurd situation. The phrase itself is quite quotable.

jokes.text: I'm a little confused too! How do I... you know, with the tail and all?
jokes.setup: Romantic moment with mermaid
jokes.punchline: Fry awkwardly addresses anatomical logistics
jokes.explanation: Standard mermaid anatomy joke handled with Fry's characteristic awkwardness.

jokes.text: Why couldn't she be the other kind of mermaid... with fish part on top, and lady part on bottom?
jokes.setup: Fry learns about fish reproduction method
jokes.punchline: He wishes for the reverse mermaid configuration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reversal of mermaid concept. Highly quotable and visually hilarious to imagine.

jokes.text: Turns out I loved her, but I wasn't in love with her.
jokes.setup: Question about what happened with the mermaid romance
jokes.punchline: Fry gives a mature relationship analysis
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly mature relationship insight from Fry, subverting his usual immaturity.

jokes.text: Wait! This is no record! Give me back that check! I'm giving it to some giant orphans.
jokes.setup: Bender receives award for record fish
jokes.punchline: Official discovers error and mentions giving money to giant orphans instead
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about 'giant orphans' as alternative charity recipients.

jokes.text: Hey, I'm trying to sleep in here!
jokes.setup: Bender being squeezed by official
jokes.punchline: The giant fish inside Bender complains about the disturbance
jokes.explanation: Good callback revealing the fish is still alive inside Bender, complaining like a tenant.

jokes.text: Hail Atlanta!
jokes.setup: Fish speaking from inside Bender
jokes.punchline: Fish gives Atlanta salute, revealing it's also from there
jokes.explanation: Nice callback ending tying the fish to the Atlanta storyline with economy.

tmdb_overview: A fishing trip over the ocean takes a turn for the worse when a colossal mouth bass pulls the Planet Express ship to the bottom of the sea. There, Fry falls in love with a mermaid named Umbriel, and the crew discovers the sunken city of Atlanta, Georgia.
